data.title: Bitcoin recovery predicted to be slow and painful by analysts, expecting a choppy correction; S&P 500 may trigger a 15%-20% market sell-off.
data.description: Prominent cryptocurrency traders Trader Mayne and DonAlt are maintaining a bearish-to-neutral stance on Bitcoin (CRYPTO: BTC), predicting a slow and grinding correction rather than a quick recovery. They expressed concerns in their latest podcast about the unlikely possibility of a sharp V-shaped rebound. Both analysts anticipate a prolonged and choppy correction due to broader macro risks, including potential equity market weakness. Trader Mayne highlighted the S&P 500's potential topping structure resembling previous market peaks and cautioned that a 15%–20% stock market dip could result in a significant crypto sell-off. Mayne suggested that a confirmed bottom may not be reached until later.

data.title: APT Price Prediction: RSI at 24 signals oversold at $0.91. Short-term target $1.10-$1.26 by March 2026.
data.description: A cryptocurrency called Aptos (APT) is currently trading at $0.91, indicating oversold conditions as per its Relative Strength Index (RSI) of 24. Despite a bearish trend, technical analysis predicts a possible rebound to a range between $1.10 and $1.26 within the next 30 days.

data.title: Equinix (NASDAQ: EQIX) beats Q4 FFO estimates at $8.91/share, Q4 sales miss at $2.420 billion. Forecasts Q1 sales $2.496-$2.536 billion, above analyst estimates. Strong Q4 performance.
data.description: Equinix Inc (EQIX) exceeded expectations in their fourth-quarter FFO results, reporting $8.91 per share compared to the estimated $8.85. However, their quarterly sales of $2.420 billion fell short of the projected $2.461 billion. Despite this, Equinix anticipates first-quarter sales to range between $2.496 billion and $2.536 billion, surpassing the predicted $2.463 billion. The company attributed their success to strong execution in Q4, reflecting a prosperous year and heightened demand for their solutions shown through increased bookings and recurring revenue. They are optimistic about achieving substantial revenue and AFFO per share growth.

data.title: Shopify beats Q4 expectations, with GMV of $123.8B and revenue of $3.7B. Analysts cut price targets but maintain positive ratings.
data.description: Shopify Inc (NASDAQ: SHOP) saw a decline in shares despite a positive fourth-quarter report. Analyst reactions included: Wedbush cut price target to $160, Goldman Sachs to $168, Needham maintained at $180, and JPMorgan reduced to $146. Shopify's GMV was $123.8 billion, up 31.1% YoY, exceeding consensus. Revenue was $3.7 billion, up 30.6% YoY, beating expectations by 2%. Q1 revenue growth is expected in low 30%. Perceived AI risks in eCommerce models exist according to analysts. Shopify's full story can be found on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Madagascar lifts 16-year mining permit suspension, allowing foreign investments; 1,650 permits pending in 2023.
data.description: An announcement by the Minister of Mines and Strategic Resources of Madagascar reveals the lifting of a 16-year suspension on the issuance of mining permits in the country, except for gold mining permits. The new system for granting permits in Madagascar includes authorizations for licenses for small-scale operators, competitive tenders for industrial projects, and involvement of the Mining Brigade in reviewing applications. Madagascar holds significant mineral resources such as nickel, cobalt, and graphite, crucial in the global energy transition. With 1,650 mining permits pending approval in 2023, this development opens up opportunities for foreign investment. The Chairman of Global Li-Ion Graphite Corp. expressed optimism, stating that this move greatly benefits the mining industry in Madagascar, allowing foreign capital to confidently invest in projects like Global Li-Ion’s Ambato-Arana Graphite mine.

data.title: Crisp launches AI Agents to boost shareholder value for retail, providing real-time insights to improve strategies.
data.description: Bentonville, Arkansas - Crisp, a prominent vertical AI platform for retail, has launched Crisp AI Agents for the retail sector, now available for general use. This platform integrates Agentic AI with extensive retail knowledge to assist brands and retailers in swiftly enhancing retail strategies using live data from all stores and SKUs. The specialized Crisp AI Agents are designed to address the most difficult daily obstacles in retail, offering analysis and actionable insights.

data.title: Veryon partners with Saudia Group for fleet analytics in passenger and cargo operations. #AI #businesswire
data.description: In San Francisco, it was reported that Veryon has entered into a partnership with Saudia Group to provide enhanced fleet analytics services for both its passenger and cargo operations. The agreement is set to strengthen Saudia Group's operational efficiency and strategy implementation.

data.title: Ciena releases 2026 Annual Meeting proxy materials online for the meeting on March 26 at 3:00 PM ET.
data.description: A company has released proxy materials for its 2026 Annual Meeting of Stockholders in Hanover, Maryland. The meeting is scheduled to take place on Thursday, March 26, 2026, at 3:00 PM Eastern Time. Shareholders are encouraged to review the materials before the meeting.

data.title: ISG launching study on specialty analytics, AI for insurers to enhance profitability and trust. #Insurance #AI
data.description: A research study by ISG has been initiated to focus on analytics and AI specialist providers aiding insurers in enhancing profitability, optimizing operations, and building trust. The study aims to delve into the capabilities and offerings of these providers in the insurance sector. This research will analyze key aspects such as their impact on boosting profitability and improving operational efficiency. The study by ISG is set to provide valuable insights into the role of analytics and AI in transforming the insurance industry, and the benefits they bring in terms of profitability, operational streamlining, and enhancing trust among stakeholders.

data.title: Terray Therapeutics launches EMMI Predict: TerraBind, boasting best-in-class accuracy and 26x faster performance in small molecule drug development.
data.description: Terray Therapeutics, a biotech company located in Los Angeles, has announced the launch of EMMI Predict: TerraBind. This is a universal potency model specifically designed for small molecule drug development. The model has been created at a scale that is crucial for the development process. This innovation marks a significant step forward for the company in its efforts to enhance drug development.

data.title: Experian launches Insurance Marketplace app on ChatGPT platform for enhanced user experience.
data.description: Experian has introduced the Experian Insurance Marketplace app on the ChatGPT platform in Costa Mesa, California. This move aims to enhance accessibility to insurance options.

data.title: Highspot and Seismic plan to merge, combining their enabling platforms to accelerate revenue growth for GTM teams. No specific numbers provided in the excerpt.
data.description: In a recent announcement, Highspot and Seismic have finalized a deal to merge their companies, forming a powerhouse in the industry. This merger aims to combine their leading enablement platforms and customer networks to enhance performance for revenue teams facing challenging market conditions. The agreement is pending closure, pending customary conditions and regulatory approvals. Upon completion, the merged entity will operate as a unified force in the market, poised to navigate the complexities of the go-to-market landscape.

data.title: AppLovin reported $1.66B revenue in Q4, exceeds estimates. Expects $1.75-1.78B revenue in Q1. Shares fell 7.3% pre-market. Analysts revised price targets.
data.description: AppLovin Corp (NASDAQ: APP) announced strong financial results for Q4, exceeding market expectations. The company recorded $1.66 billion in revenue, surpassing analyst predictions of $1.60 billion. Earnings per share for the quarter were $3.24, higher than the estimated $2.95. Projections for Q1 indicate revenue between $1.75 billion and $1.78 billion, while adjusted EBITDA is expected to be around $1.47 billion to $1.50 billion. Despite the positive results, AppLovin's shares dropped by 7.3% to $423.55 in pre-market trading. Analysts have adjusted their price targets for the company post-earnings. For more details, the full story is available on Benzinga.com.
